To emulate the delete confirmation behavior in the Django admin when saving certain models, you can leverage the `ModelAdmin` class and override the `response_change` or `response_delete` methods. These methods allow you to customize the behavior after an action is performed. For cascading deletions, you can check if any dependent objects will be affected and prompt the user for confirmation. However, Django’s admin does not natively support a confirmation dialog for save operations, so you may need to implement a custom template or use JavaScript to display a confirmation modal. This approach ensures that users are aware of the consequences before proceeding.
